pattern: Binary search on even indices exploits that pairs stay aligned before the singleton and shift afterward, locating it in logarithmic time.
hints: Before the lone element each pair starts at an even index; after it the alignment breaks.
Compare the element at an even mid with its right neighbor to decide which half holds the singleton.
Search on even indices: if nums[mid] equals nums[mid+1] the singleton is to the right, otherwise it is at or left of mid; converge to a single even index.

stress.description: construct a sorted array of pairs with one inserted singleton
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    pairs = max(0, size)
    vals = sorted(rng.sample(range(0, 100001), pairs + 1))
    single = vals.pop(rng.randrange(len(vals)))
    arr = []
    for v in vals:
        arr.append(v)
        arr.append(v)
    arr.append(single)
    arr.sort()
    return [arr]
